How to Use the Name Popularity Checker

A complete six-step guide to extracting maximum insight from the Name Popularity Checker.

New here? Start with the Popularity Checker itself, then return for the deeper walkthrough below.

What does the Name Popularity Checker do?

The Name Popularity Checker takes any first name and returns five attributes from official birth-registry data: global rank, estimated living bearers, decade-by-decade trend, gender distribution and country breakdown. This guide walks you through reading each metric in under five minutes. Our data is aggregated from international authorities like the UN Statistics Division and World Health Organization life tables. Want to see a practical example? Head to the homepage to find out how many people have your name.

1

Enter a Name

Type any first name into the search box. Our tool accepts names from any origin, language, or cultural tradition.

2

View the Results

You'll see the estimated number of people with that name, its global popularity rank, and a gender distribution breakdown.

3

Explore Historical Trends

The decade popularity chart shows how the name's popularity has changed from the 1880s to today.

4

Check Regional Data

See how the name is distributed across the US, UK, Canada, Australia and 76 other countries.

5

Discover Similar Names

Find related names, alternative spellings, and names with similar origins or meanings.

6

Compare Names

Use the comparison tool to see two names side by side with statistical analysis.

Tips for Best Results

  • Use the standard spelling for the most accurate aggregated data
  • Try alternative spellings to compare variants (e.g. Catherine vs Katherine)
  • Read the decade chart to see whether a name is rising, falling or stable
  • Use the comparison tool when deciding between two finalists
  • Use the Trend Visualizer for 3- or 4-name multi-line comparisons

What the Checker Tells You

Global rank

Ordinal position out of all recorded first names worldwide.

Estimated bearer count

Number of living people who share the name, adjusted via actuarial life tables.

Decade popularity score

0–100 normalized score for each decade from 1880s to 2020s.

Gender distribution

Percentage split between male, female and unisex usage.

Country breakdown

Per-country bearer counts for 80+ nations.

Direct name page link

Each lookup links to a dedicated SEO-optimized name page with all metrics.

Who Uses This Tool?

First-time tool users

Scenario: Want to understand what every metric on the result card means.

Result: Walk through this 6-step guide once and you'll read future results in seconds.

Researchers citing the data

Scenario: Need to know the data sources for academic publication.

Result: Step-by-step covers SSA, ONS and methodology — link in your citation.

Parents comparing 5+ names

Scenario: Have a long shortlist and need an efficient workflow.

Result: Guide explains when to use Popularity Checker vs Comparison vs Trend Visualizer for max efficiency.

Casual curious users

Scenario: Just want to check their own name once.

Result: Steps 1–2 are enough; the rest unlocks deeper analysis if you want it.

✓ Pros

  • ✓ Six clear steps — readable in under 5 minutes
  • ✓ Includes data-source explanation
  • ✓ Cross-links to companion tools (Comparison, Trend Visualizer)
  • ✓ Free guide, free tool, no signup
  • ✓ Works as both a tutorial and a reference

✗ Cons

  • ✗ Step 4 (regional data) requires opening the full name page — not on the checker card
  • ✗ No video walkthrough yet (roadmap)
  • ✗ Methodology details are deeper on the dedicated /methodology page

Country breakdowns✓ 80+ countriesUS onlyMostly US/UKUS only
Free + no signup✓LimitedPremium-gated✓
Bearer-count estimates✓ With life-table adjustment✗✗✗

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the Name Popularity Checker?▾

It's a free tool that returns a name's global rank, estimated bearer count, gender split, decade trends and country distribution — all from official birth-registry data.

What data sources does the checker use?▾

U.S. Social Security Administration (1880–present), UK Office for National Statistics, Statistics Canada, plus aggregated census data from 78 additional countries.

How accurate is the popularity data?▾

For a name with more than 1,000 bearers, estimates are typically within ±5% of the true count, calculated using actuarial life tables on historical birth data.

Why use the spelling that's most common?▾

Statistical aggregation works best on the canonical spelling. 'Catherine' and 'Katherine' are tracked separately — pick the one most relevant to you.

Can I check uncommon or international names?▾

Yes. The database covers 100,000+ names across 80+ countries. Even rare names return a 'likely fewer than X bearers' estimate.

How often is the data updated?▾

Annually, every January, with the previous calendar year's birth registry release.

Is the guide and tool free?▾

Yes — both are free, unlimited and require no signup.

What's the difference between rank and count?▾

Rank is the name's position in the all-time ordered list (e.g. #15). Count is the estimated number of living people with the name (e.g. ~580k).

Does the tool work on mobile?▾

Yes — fully responsive, works on iOS and Android browsers.

Can I share my result?▾

Yes, name pages have stable URLs you can copy and share.
